title: Fix git mutation guard canonical archive push block

tasks.title: Add failing allow/deny regression tests for canonical archive mutation on default branch versus unrelated default-branch git mutation.
tasks.verification: Regression coverage added. Red phase could not be reproduced because current trunk-write firewall already allows git commands, including canonical archive push; focused tests prove allow path and existing deny cases remain covered.
tasks.summary: Added trunk-write-firewall unit regression and integration hook regression for `git -C <repo> push origin main` from default-branch checkout. Existing destructive write deny tests remain in place.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added trunk-write-firewall unit regression and integration hook regression for `git -C <repo> push origin main` from default-branch checkout. Existing destructive write deny tests remain in place.

tasks.title: Implement narrow auditable archive-operation allow path in git mutation guard while preserving unrelated default-branch mutation blocking.
tasks.verification: No production-code mutation required: retired git-guard no longer exists in plugin/src, and trunk-write-firewall already allows all git commands while blocking direct destructive file writes to trunk. Regression tests now lock canonical archive push allow path plus existing deny behavior.
tasks.summary: Preserved current trunk-write-firewall implementation. The safe fix is regression coverage, not reintroducing a git-command classifier or broad archive allowlist.
tasks.implementation_summary: Preserved current trunk-write-firewall implementation. The safe fix is regression coverage, not reintroducing a git-command classifier or broad archive allowlist.

tasks.title: Run focused git guard/archive tests and plugin check; document verification evidence.
tasks.verification: pnpm vitest run src/tools/trunk-write-firewall.test.ts src/integration.test.ts --reporter=verbose; pnpm run check

proposal.approval_evidence: Approved via structured ADV_ATC_RESPONSE on GitHub issue #102.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery completed from issue #102, proposal, and project rules. Core fact: default-branch mutation guard must preserve trunk-is-prod safety while allowing the specific canonical archive operation. No conflict or user tradeoff found.

design.approval_evidence: Design completed autonomously. Plan uses narrow archive-operation allow path plus allow/deny regression tests. No HITL tradeoff, conflict, or contract compromise.

planning.approval_evidence: Planning approved via structured ADV_ATC_RESPONSE on GitHub issue #102.

execution.approval_evidence: Execution complete. Added regression coverage proving canonical archive push command from trunk checkout passes through trunk-write-firewall while existing direct file-write/destructive bash deny tests remain. No production code change required because retired git-guard no longer exists and current firewall deliberately allows git commands. Verification: `pnpm vitest run src/tools/trunk-write-firewall.test.ts src/integration.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed (49 tests); `pnpm run check` passed. Checkpoint commit 580a98ea68f092ce5b0d77a1bf9b27a4bbcccd5a.

acceptance.approval_evidence: Batch acceptance approved via GH ATC_RESPONSE for all bug changes

release.approval_evidence: Release approved via structured ADV_ATC_RESPONSE on GitHub issue #102.

clarify_findings.message: Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
